# Traction Lithium Battery Recovery
Web-based functional model for traction lithium battery recovery

## Description
Reference functional model for the activities and processes involved in recovering traction lithium batteries at the end of their use/life. The model includes inputs and outputs of each activity, as well as factors that constrain and enable the activities (controls and mechanisms), using the IDEF0 methodology. It provides a unified point of reference that enables stakeholders to identify new opportunities, inform strategic decision-making, and drive innovation within the emerging battery recovery industry. The model is available as downloadable files and hosted on a website to view and explore directly.

## Contact
Matthew Triebe
  matthew.triebe@nist.gov

## Website
Visit the linked website to access and explore the model. DOI: https://doi.org/10.18434/mds2-4191
To host the website locally:
1. Download or clone the repository
2. Navigate to your repository in the command line
3. Follow the instructions from the jekyll documentation to ensure you have all prerequisites installed: [https://jekyllrb.com/docs/](https://jekyllrb.com/docs/)
4. Install Github-approved gems: gem install github-pages
5. Run the following command: jekyll serve
6. Navigate to the Server Address shown in your terminal.

## Model Files
To view the model locally:
1. Download the TLB-R_offline.zip file and unzip it.
2. Open the index.html in a web browser.
3. On the left hand side, click the "Treat Traction Lithium Batteries at End-of-Life" link to open the model.
4. Click on the activities (boxes) to see descriptions and to decompose the activity.
5. Scroll down in the description frame (lower frame) to find the Decoposition link.
6. To move up the model, scroll down in the main frame (top right) to find the Parent Activity link.
7. Activities with a shadow have decompositions. Activities without a shadow may still have descriptions, but no further detail is provided.

Alternatively:
1. Download the 'tlb/tlb' folder to access the files for the Traction Lithium Battery Recovery Model in html format.
2. View the 'tlb/tlb/images' folder to view images of the system diagrams.
